reflection.quote: The most regretful people on earth are those who felt the call to creative work, who felt their own creative power restive and uprising, and gave to it neither power nor time.
reflection.reading:
Sundays used to be a war zone. The hangover, the dread of Monday, the slow accounting of what I'd said and to whom, the promises to myself that I already knew I'd break by Thursday. Sunday was the day the bill came due, and I paid it in shame and Gatorade and another vow to get my act together.

Now Sunday is just Sunday. Coffee that I actually taste. A long walk where my head isn't pounding. A phone call with someone I love where I'm not performing wellness, I just am well. These are the hours I would have traded anything for back then, and I didn't even know it. I thought the good life was loud. Turns out a lot of it is quiet.

Mary Oliver was talking about creative work, but I think the same is true of a life. The regret isn't usually about the big swings you didn't take — it's about the ordinary power you had and didn't use. The Sunday you could have shown up for and didn't. Sobriety hands you back your Sundays. What you do with them is the whole point. Read the book. Call the friend. Make the eggs. The life you're building isn't somewhere else. It's right here, in the hours nobody is watching.
reflection.meditation: close your eyes for a moment.with minute. Notice this hour. Notice that you are in it, not recovering from the last one or bracing for the next.dread Whatever this Sunday holds — small errands, a walk, a meal, nothing at all — it belongs to you now. That is not nothing. That is what you fought for., today.,.. and you,.not.notice notice.. Notice. Sit with the simple fact that you are here, sober, on an ordinary day. That is the foundation everything else gets built on.tabled.tabled.tabled..

affirmations: I am building a life out of ordinary hours, and that is enough.
Today I get to be present for things I used to sleep through.
I have agency over how I spend this day, and I choose to spend it awake.

gratitude.prayer: I'm grateful for a quiet Sunday and a clear head to enjoy it. I'm grateful for the version of me who kept showing up long enough to get here, and for the people who showed up alongside him. The small things — coffee, sunlight, a phone that rings with friends instead of bills — these used to feel out of reach. Today they're just my life.
gratitude.closing: One more day, awake for it. That's the whole gift.
